DISCOVERY 302, ALL PARTIES PRESENT STIPULATE TO JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE HEARING TODAY'S MOTION. ALL PARTIES AND JUDGE PRO TEM AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE STIPULATION ON THEIR OWN BEHALF. HEARING IS HELD. ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. PLAINTIFF GEORGINA VALDEZ'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES FROM DEFENDANT FCA US LLC, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT IS ADVISED THAT SEVERAL OF THE OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY ISSUES HAVE BEEN RESOLVED. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 45-48 WERE NOT RESOLVED. THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THOSE INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED SUBJECT TO THE NARROWING OF THE INTERROGATORIES. DEFENSE COUNSEL IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO JUDGE PRO TEM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM. PROPOSED ORDER IS TO BE SERVED ON PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL FOR APPROVAL AS TO FROM. PLAINTIFF MAY SUBMIT A COMPETING ORDER TO JUDGE PRO TEM COHEN IF APPROVAL OF DEFENSE COUNSEL'S ORDER IS NOT AGREED TO. JUDGE PRO TEM: TOMAS COHEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/JPT)

All parties present stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne hearing today's motion. All parties and Judge Pro Tem authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation on their own behalf.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules adopts the tentative ruling as modified.

PLAINTIFF GEORGINA VALDEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Special Interrogatories From Defendant Fca Us Llc, And Request For Sanctions

The Court is advised that several of the outstanding discovery issues have been resolved. Special Interrogatories 45-48 were not resolved. The Motion to compel further responses to those interrogatories is granted subject to the narrowing of the interrogatories.

Defense counsel is to prepare and submit a proposed order to Judge Pro Tem Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com. Proposed order is to be served on plaintiffs counsel for approval as to from. Plaintiff may submit a competing order to Judge Pro Tem Cohen if approval of defense counsel's order is not agreed to.

Judge Pro Tem: Tomas Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
